title: Free AI tools every PhD student should know in 2025
post:
Being a PhD student on a budget doesn't mean you can't leverage AI. Here are the free tools I rely on: ChatGPT's free tier for brainstorming and drafting, Claude for longer analytical conversations, Perplexity for literature searches, and Zotero's AI features for citation management.

I've also found that many universities now have institutional AI licenses that students overlook. Check with your library or IT department — you might have access to more powerful tools than you realize.

The researchers who thrive in the next few years won't be the ones with the biggest grants. They'll be the ones who learn to use these tools effectively.

What free AI tools have you found essential for grad school? Thoughts?
